What changes a shipping rate

Four inputs shape the landed cost.

Route & zone

Local, regional, metro-to-metro and remote-area lanes carry different network costs.

Billable weight

Couriers commonly charge the higher of actual weight and calculated volumetric weight.

Service speed

Priority air or express movement typically costs more than economy surface shipping.

Payment mode

COD shipments may include collection, remittance or percentage-based handling charges.

Remote-area access

Extended delivery areas can add handling charges depending on the courier network.

Protection needs

Declared value, risk coverage and special handling can change the final shipment cost.

Pricing questions

Understand the estimate before you ship.

These answers explain common differences between an estimate and a final courier charge.

Volumetric weight converts the space a parcel occupies into a billable weight using its packed length, width and height.

It is an indicative starting estimate. Final cost depends on verified dimensions, courier selection, service mode and applicable surcharges.

COD can include a fixed or percentage-based collection charge because the courier handles payment and remittance.

Yes. Share your route mix, weight profile and monthly volume with our team for a tailored commercial review.

Need a route-wise commercial plan?

Send us your shipment profile and our team will help build a more accurate volume proposal.
